sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 81:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 81:1
items.html: When the light departs from the vessel and the coupling is made in the degree above it, such as when it departs from phase three, and the coupling is made in phase two, then the posterior of this vessel, which is phase two, looks at the upper light, meaning receives from the illumination of the coupling that is there. (Inner Light, Item‎ 15)
